overall_summary: Meta supports large load interconnection reform but opposes binding detailed rules, instead advocating for guidance and best practices that preserve private-sector innovation. The company calls for raising the minimum MW threshold to 75 MW or higher, expanding expedited interconnection for co-located resources regardless of curtailment requirements, and clarifying cost allocation and operational procedures for hybrid facilities.

quotes.text: the Commission should consider adopting guidance, best practices, and, if appropriate, minimum standards for large load interconnection rather than promulgating a binding, detailed rule
quotes.concern: Oppose binding rule; favor guidance instead

quotes.text: Adopting guidance would encourage uniformity in industry practices while allowing for continued private-sector innovation and accommodating the varying stages of data center deployment across the country
Data center developers and other sources of large load need faster, transparent, and more predictable interconnection processes

The Commission should therefore consider developing minimum standards for the interconnection of large loads and encouraging greater uniformity in the processes that utilities, grid operators, and regulators use to bring data centers online

The Commission, however, should not rush to impose a one-size-fits-all regulatory solution nationwide. Some regions of the country are just beginning to bring data centers online, while others have already interconnected substantial large data center loads and are working quickly to add more. Keeping this momentum going is imperative. Issuing a detailed, standard rule that fails to account for the diversity in the economic landscape could slow down successful interconnection processes

In Meta's experience, that threshold is too low and would not accurately target the large loads that would most benefit from reform of interconnection processes. Meta proposes that the Commission instead consider a threshold of 75 MW or higher
quotes.concern: Raise threshold from 20 MW to 75 MW or higher

quotes.text: Meta strongly supports expedited review of facilities that co-locate large loads and generation. Co-location allows large loads to secure electric power quickly, and, in some instances, streamline the build out of necessary transmission infrastructure
quotes.concern: Support expedited review for co-located facilities

quotes.text: Meta also agrees with the ANOPR's recommendation in paragraph 20 that large load be studied together with generating facilities such as by studying 'the composite behavior of co-located load and generation' so long as joint study processes do not delay the interconnection of large loads
quotes.concern: Support joint study if no delays to load interconnection

quotes.text: utilities should not limit expedited processes to only large loads or hybrid facilities 'that agree to be curtailable', which is discussed in paragraph 24 of the ANOPR. This limitation would be incompatible with the operational requirements of many data centers, which often allow for little downtime over the course of the year
quotes.concern: Oppose curtailment requirement for expedited access

quotes.text: Coordination of new load and incremental new generation allows data centers to meet their power demands without resorting to curtailment commitments and therefore warrants expedited study
quotes.concern: Expedited study for non-curtailable coordinated loads

quotes.text: Meta agrees that interconnection of hybrid facilities should require installation of 'the system protection facilities necessary to prevent unauthorized injections or withdrawals that exceed [those] respective rights'
the Commission should clarify that large load and generation sharing a common point of interconnection do not require additional regulatory hurdles solely due to their co-located configuration
quotes.concern: Eliminate extra hurdles for co-located configs

quotes.text: when generation to power large load is sited behind the meter, regulators should use cost-allocation principles that ensure no negative impacts to grid reliability or unfair cost shifts to other customers
quotes.concern: Fair cost allocation for behind-meter generation

quotes.text: the Commission should call on utilities and grid operators to promptly develop streamlined study processes to study large load and incremental generation simultaneously and on an expedited basis
quotes.concern: Streamline concurrent study of load and generation

quotes.text: the Commission should clarify the conditions under which co-located load and new generation may be sited and initially operated without utility or grid operator approval, in advance of later connection to the grid pursuant to interconnection procedures

bins.name: Guidance over binding rules
bins.description: Meta supports adoption of guidance and best practices rather than detailed binding rules for large load interconnection. The company argues that guidance encourages uniformity while allowing private-sector innovation and accommodation of varying regional circumstances. Imposing one-size-fits-all rules risks slowing successful interconnection processes.

bins.name: Raise minimum threshold to 75 MW
bins.description: Meta proposes raising the minimum MW threshold for large load interconnection reform from the ANOPR's proposed 20 MW to 75 MW or higher, arguing that 20 MW does not accurately target loads that would benefit from reform.

bins.name: Expedited study without curtailment
bins.description: Meta strongly supports expedited interconnection for co-located large loads and generation but opposes conditioning expedited treatment on curtailment requirements. The company argues that data centers require firm continuous power and that coordination of load and generation enables them to meet power demands without curtailment commitments.

bins.name: Co-location and coordinated generation
bins.description: Meta advocates for streamlined procedures for co-located large loads and generation, including elimination of additional regulatory hurdles, fair cost-allocation principles for behind-meter siting, concurrent expedited study processes, and clarity on pre-grid-connection deployment without prior utility approval.

bins.name: Joint study of load and generation
bins.description: Meta agrees that large loads should be studied jointly with generating facilities, using composite behavior analysis, provided that joint study processes do not delay large load interconnection.

bins.name: System protection requirements
bins.description: Meta supports installation of system protection facilities to prevent unauthorized injections or withdrawals exceeding respective interconnection rights for hybrid facilities.

bins.name: Flexibility for operational requirements
bins.description: Meta opposes curtailment requirements as conditions for expedited treatment, arguing that data center operational requirements typically allow little downtime and that backup generation has regulatory constraints limiting its use for non-emergency demand response.

bins.name: Fair cost allocation
bins.description: Meta calls for cost-allocation principles that ensure no negative grid reliability impacts or unfair cost shifts to other customers when generation powering large loads is sited behind the meter.

bins.name: Pre-grid-connection operational rights
bins.description: Meta seeks clarity on the conditions under which co-located load and generation can be sited and initially operated without utility or grid operator approval prior to formal interconnection.
